title: Which record player is right for me?
author: Electriz TechTalk
excerpt: It shouldn't be news by now that vinyl is incredibly popular. But what you might not know is that vinyl just hit a 20-year sales high in 2025. And...
content:
It shouldn't be news by now that vinyl is incredibly popular. But what you might not know is that vinyl just hit a 20-year sales high in 2025. And it's not reissues of Pink Floyd either. The best-selling vinyl stars of 2024 were Taylor Swift, the Weeknd and Sabrina Carpenter. Owning physical music is most definitely back!

So whether you're brand new to vinyl, you've got a collection you want to dig out and play, or you're looking for a new deck for a DJ set, you'll need the right record player. Not to worry. We know a thing or two about spinning discs and we've got a brilliant selection of turntables too.

## How to choose the right record player

Selecting a record player comes down to: what you'd like to use it for, preferred features and how much you've budgeted. Turntables range from budget-friendly models to high-end systems.

## For the beginner - Sony PS-LX310BT Belt Drive Bluetooth Turntable

A stellar pick for vinyl newbies. It has a pre-amp built in, Bluetooth connectivity, sturdy aluminium tonearm, and it's fully automatic -- the tonearm resets when the record finishes.

## For the DJ - Pioneer DJ PLX-500 Direct Drive Turntable

Its high-quality needle and cartridge give professional level sound. The direct drive motor responds quickly when mixing and scratching. USB port for recording to laptop, plus included Rekordbox software.

## For the audiophile - Lenco LBT-345 Belt Drive Bluetooth Turntable

Combining classic turntable charm with Bluetooth convenience. Equipped with an Audio-Technica cartridge for top-notch sound quality, audio conversion software for digitising records, and gain control for fine-tuning.

## Turntable features to consider before buying

**Drive type**: Belt drives reduce vibrations for clearer sound; direct drives offer more control and are preferred by DJs.

**Speed options**: Look for 33 1/3 and 45 RPM support. Some players handle 78s too.

**Cartridge**: Models like Audio-Technica are renowned for precision and durability.

**Output connectivity**: Bluetooth is convenient; RCA outputs ensure compatibility with many audio systems.

## Vinyl care tips

**Cleaning**: Use a carbon fibre brush before and after each play. For deeper cleaning, use a record cleaning solution.

**Storage**: Keep records in dust sleeves, store vertically, away from sunlight and extreme temperatures.

**Maintenance**: Regularly clean the stylus with a soft brush. Check cartridge alignment. Replace the stylus when it shows wear.

## Budget vs premium turntables

Budget turntables offer decent sound for casual listeners, with built-in preamps and speakers. Premium models use high-mass platters and precision-engineered tonearms with durable materials like aluminium, steel or wood.
